scores.honest_pure_c_floor_note: Both head_floor and candidate_floor are now 7 because the cleaned candidate.c (Region A named-base lever applied, the inherited inner `do { } while (0);` REMOVED per session-2 cheat-reviewer FAIL) measures floor 7 (build_insns 81 vs target 82). The Region A lever DID measurably lower the true pure-C floor: HEAD's TRUE pure-C floor (no inner do-while + no Region A lever) is 9; candidate's TRUE pure-C floor (Region A lever + no inner do-while) is 7. The +2 improvement in candidate_floor=5 that the prior session reported reflected the as-written candidate including the inherited sched1-fence cheat; that floor depended on the cheat and is no longer the correct baseline.

sessions.agent: headless worker (opus), batch bfej90w2j iter 1; checkpoint authored by orchestrator after layer-2 reviewer FAIL revert of 4dd017d8
sessions.lever: Region A (named-base intermediate): split `frame = (D_800A151C + a0) - 1` into `s32 base = D_800A151C - 1; frame = base + a0;` — defeats combine's CSE of the shared `a0 - 1` subexpression with the sibling `count = a0 - 1`, so the addiu lands before the j and the addu in the delay slot, matching target. Retires the first regfix reorder rule (TEXT 23-25). SOTN-accepted named-intermediate family; layer-2 reviewer PASSed this construct.
sessions.note_on_floor: floor_after of 5 was the as-written engine measurement WITH the inherited inner do-while in source. Session 2's cheat-reviewer FAILed that body; candidate.c was cleaned and the corrected floor is 7. See sessions[1].

sessions.agent: headless worker (opus), follow-up session resuming from orchestrator's WIP
sessions.lever: Cleaned the candidate: removed the inherited inner `do { } while (0);` at candidate.c line 30 per cheat-reviewer FAIL (sched1-fence cheat, out of scope for do-while-zero-exception). Ruled out 2 hypotheses from the WIP's next_hypotheses with measurements (block-hoist arg, shared volatile pointer). New honest floor is 7 (build_insns 81 vs target 82). Region A lever still PASSes adversarial review.
sessions.floor_change_note: The floor RISE (5 -> 7) reflects removing an inherited sched1-fence cheat from candidate.c, NOT a regression in the C structure. Region A lever's measurable benefit (9 -> 7 vs TRUE HEAD pure-C floor) is preserved. The +2 'improvement' the prior session reported (7 -> 5) was a tainted measurement that depended on the cheat.

hypotheses_tested.name: hypothesis 1 — hoist g_sys_dma_region+1 into a named local declared FIRST in a block surrounding the s0 reload + second call
hypotheses_tested.result: WORSE versus the WITH-cheat baseline 5 (now moot). Hoisting biased sched1 to schedule the arg load EARLY (low LUID for dma_arg), making `addiu a0,a0,1` land BEFORE `lw s0` and `li a1,1` end up in the jal delay slot. Target wants the OPPOSITE: s0 load FIRST, then arg setup, then jal with `addiu a0,a0,1` in delay slot. The rule's documented symptom (fill_delay + delete + reorder cluster around a pre-call STORE) does not match this function's gap (single 7-index reorder around a pre-call LOAD).
hypotheses_tested.needs_remeasurement_vs_clean_baseline: yes — measured against tainted baseline; should be re-tested against the new clean floor-7 baseline before final ruling. The CONCLUSION (sched1 priority points the wrong direction for this lever) is likely robust, but the score delta vs the new baseline has not been measured.

hypotheses_tested.name: hypothesis 2 — shared volatile s32 *ptr declared before the s0 reload, used pre-call AND post-call
hypotheses_tested.result: CATASTROPHICALLY WORSE — cc1 cached the pointer in a callee-save register across the call, eliminating the post-call lui+lw reload but breaking the entire region's structure. Target reloads D_800A1510 fresh both pre-call and post-call (lines 44-47 and 57-58 of asm/funcs/sys_VSync.s). Conclusion robust against baseline change: caching the pointer across the call is fundamentally wrong for matching target's reload pattern.

hypotheses_tested.name: variant — scoped volatile s32 *p just for the pre-call s0 read (single-use, local block)
hypotheses_tested.result: NO CHANGE versus the inner-do-while-removed baseline — cc1 inlines the local pointer. Same floor 7.

hypotheses_tested.name: variant — function-scope `s32 dma` local, assignment at use site
hypotheses_tested.form: Declare `s32 dma;` at function top; `dma = g_sys_dma_region + 1;` just before the call
hypotheses_tested.result: NO CHANGE versus tainted baseline — cc1 collapses the function-scope local to the same RTL as the inline expression. Score 5 preserved with the inherited cheat; now moot.

cheat_reviewer_verdict_on_prior_candidat.summary: Cheat-reviewer FAILed the as-written candidate body because the inherited inner `do { } while (0);` at candidate.c line 30 is a sched1-fence cheat, explicitly out of scope for the do-while-zero-exception rule (which sanctions only the LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P / reorg.c invert-jump peephole on NE conditions). The construct fails the exception's prerequisites on three independent grounds: (1) wrong GCC pass (sched1, not reorg.c); (2) no `/* FAKE: ... */` annotation; (3) no lever-exhaustion documentation (introduced commit d7c91eba 2026-04-09, pre-dating the rule). Same family as the outer do-while that was FAILed in commit 4dd017d8's retro-audit (rejected/do-while-sched1-fence.c). Region A named-base lever and the spin-wait `volatile s32 *ptr` block were independently judged PASS.
cheat_reviewer_verdict_on_prior_candidat.next_action_applied: Removed the inner do-while from candidate.c line 30. New honest baseline: floor 7 (build_insns 81 vs target 82). meta.json and notes.md updated.

discoveries.name: inner-do-while-at-line-69 confirmed as a sched1-fence cheat (RESOLVED by reviewer FAIL)
discoveries.details: Originally surfaced as a NEEDS_USER policy question. The session-2 cheat-reviewer explicitly FAILed the construct, noting the do-while-zero-exception rule's text excludes sched1 uses and lists three independent prerequisite failures. The reviewer's analysis: 'the policy answer is unambiguous: the construct fails the do-while-zero-exception prerequisites on three independent grounds and is forbidden.' Not a NEEDS_USER after all — a clear FAIL. The construct was introduced at commit d7c91eba (2026-04-09) and remained un-audited until session 2's pass. The layer-2 retro-audit of 4dd017d8 had focused on the OUTER do-while being added in that commit and did not separately evaluate the inherited inner one — confirming session 2's hypothesis that the prior reviewer's omission was an oversight, not implicit grandfathering.

sessions.lever: Executed the WIP's PRIMARY UNPROBED LEVER — built clean single-function permuter at permuter/sys_VSync/ (base.c trimmed to sys_VSync only, target.o assembled from asm/funcs/sys_VSync.s + r3000-stripped prelude.inc, sys_VSync at offset 0 in both base.o and target.o). Verified baseline permuter score = 495 (matches sandbox floor-7 character — 1 ins/del + ~6-7 reorderings). Ran random-mode permuter for ~5 minutes with -j 6 (estimated several hundred to ~1000 iterations). All non-zero-score improvements found were CHEAT-FAMILY (do-while sched1-fence wraps around call sites). No legitimate (non-cheat) form beat the 495 baseline.

permuter_findings.verdict: FAIL — same sched1-fence family as the inherited inner do-while that session-2 cheat-reviewer FAILed. Out of scope for do-while-zero-exception (which sanctions only the LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P / reorg.c invert-jump peephole on NE conditions). Saved to rejected/permuter-do-while-around-call.c.

permuter_findings.form: do { func_80082A14(g_sys_dma_region + 1, 1); } while (0); + s0_val = base; frame = s0_val + a0; (dead-store-via-s0_val-reuse in Region A)
permuter_findings.verdict: FAIL — composite cheat (sched1-fence + dead-store coercion via callee-save-pinned local reuse). Same family.

permuter_findings.form: do { if (a0 == 1) return s1_val; { Region-A block } s0_val = *D_800A1510; } while (0); — wraps the multi-statement prologue + Region A + s0_val read in a do-while-zero, leaving only the second call outside
permuter_findings.verdict: FAIL — same sched1-fence family, just a different wrapping location.

permuter_findings.form: do { { Region-A block including the first call } } while (0);
permuter_findings.verdict: FAIL — wraps the first call's block. Same family.

permuter_findings.form: s0_val = g_sys_dma_region; return s0_val; (in the a0<0 early-return path) + func_80082A14(s0_val + 1, 1); + D_800A151C = s0_val; — uses s0_val for multiple unrelated values
permuter_findings.verdict: SEMANTICALLY BROKEN — the early-return path's s0_val assignment dies (return before s0_val is used elsewhere); the call's first arg uses s0_val (= *D_800A1510 from the volatile read) instead of g_sys_dma_region + 1; the tail store also writes the wrong value. Not a closing form, just incorrect.

sessions.directed_perm_macros_status: NOT yet probed. The PERM_* macro surface in tools/decomp-permuter/USAGE.md (PERM_FORCE_FORWARD_DECL, PERM_LINESWAP, PERM_VAR, PERM_ONCE, PERM_TYPECAST, PERM_BREAK, PERM_RANDOMIZE, etc.) lets you script TARGETED mutations rather than the random-mode shotgun. A directed sweep over PERM_LINESWAP across Region B's 3 statements (s0_val read, dma_arg compute, call) + PERM_VAR over local types might surface a mutation that affects reorg.c's delay-slot fill decision rather than just sched1's LUID surface. This is the next reachable avenue.

discoveries.name: Random-permuter cheat-attraction confirms the cheat is the closing form's family
discoveries.details: Permuter's random mutations found the do-while-around-call cheat as the BEST score (40), with 4 other variants of the same family at scores 50, 300, 355. The permuter's mutation surface IS expressive enough to wrap arbitrary statement blocks in do-while-zero, but no legitimate (non-cheat) mutation gets close to even score 100. This is strong evidence that the sched1-fence IS what closes Region B; the random-permuter cannot find a non-fence form because the legitimate lever surface (reorg.c delay-slot fill) is not reached by random C-level mutations of this structure.

discoveries.name: Single-function permuter setup works (sys_VSync at offset 0 in both base.o and target.o)
discoveries.details: permuter/sys_VSync/ contains: base.c (trimmed to sys_VSync + decls only, 2.4KB), target.s (asm/funcs/sys_VSync.s + prelude.inc with .set gp=64 stripped for r3000), target.o (assembled, sys_VSync at offset 0), compile.sh (same pipeline as src/ build), base.o (built from base.c, sys_VSync at offset 0). Verified baseline score = 495 = ~95% reduced from the prior whole-file noise (15310). Future sessions can re-run permuter without rebuilding the directory.

discoveries.name: Session-1 measured negatives REMAIN un-tested at clean floor-7
discoveries.details: Per the prior WIP's next_hypothesis #5, session-1's measured negatives (operand reassociation, last-arg hoist, merged if/else, inverted branch, eager-flag pre-computation) should be re-tested against the new floor-7 baseline. Session 4 prioritised the PRIMARY UNPROBED LEVER (permuter) ahead of these. They remain on the worklist for the next session.

sessions.lever: Probed dual-named-intermediate (vs + arg) family in Region B targeting reorg.c delay-slot fill. Measured 30+ near-isomorphic shapes via tmp/sys_vsync_variants*.py sweeps. The form `{ s32 vs = *D_800A1510; s32 arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; s0_val = vs; func_80082A14(arg, 1); }` (M5) and 9 isomorphs (R1, R2, R4, R7, R8, R9, Q5, Q6, Q7) ALL hit floor 6 with build_insns 82 (== target). Variants with only `arg`, only `vs`, or with `arg` declared before `vs`, scored 7. The cheat-reviewer FAILed M5: `vs` is an identity-bounce (semantically equivalent to `s0_val = *D_800A1510;` HEAD form); NOT the SOTN named-intermediate-declaration-order family (that family names a real sub-computation; an identity bounce has no sub-computation to name). The declaration-order sensitivity is textbook GCC-LUID-ordering coercion. M5 saved to rejected/m5-vs-arg-identity-bounce.c.
sessions.floor_change_note: candidate.c (and thus candidate_floor) UNCHANGED — the M5 family that reached floor 6 was rejected by cheat-reviewer as identity-bounce/codegen-coercion. The candidate stays at the session-2 cleaned form (floor 7). The new rejected family is recorded in rejected_forms[] and rejected/m5-vs-arg-identity-bounce.c.

hypotheses_tested.name: M5 — dual named-intermediate, vs initialized first, then arg, then s0_val=vs, then call
hypotheses_tested.form: { s32 vs = *D_800A1510; s32 arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; s0_val = vs; func_80082A14(arg, 1); }
hypotheses_tested.result: MEASURED IMPROVEMENT (7 → 6, build_insns matches target). Build's `move s0, v0` (from `s0_val = vs;`) lands in jal delay slot; `addiu a0, a0, 1` ends up before the jal at d8 instead of in delay slot like target. Cheat-reviewer FAIL — see rejected/m5-vs-arg-identity-bounce.c.
hypotheses_tested.cheat_reviewer_reason: Identity bounce `vs = read; s0_val = vs` has no sub-computation to name → not the SOTN named-intermediate family. `arg` named intermediate with no reuse / no clarity gain → no semantic purpose. Declaration-order sensitivity (only vs-first works) is textbook LUID-ordering manipulation. Compare Region A's `s32 base = D_800A151C - 1` which names a real frame-offset sub-computation (PASS) — M5's intermediates name nothing.

hypotheses_tested.name: Sister forms in the same family (R1, R2, R4, R7, R8, R9, Q5, Q6, Q7)
hypotheses_tested.result: ALL same floor as M5, all same FAIL family (different syntactic spellings of the dual-intermediate-with-vs-first construct). R9 in particular uses `(s32 *)D_800A1510` (cast away volatile) as a separate cheat-family hit — flagged but not pursued.

hypotheses_tested.name: P1 — move `s0_val = vs;` to AFTER the call
hypotheses_tested.form: { s32 vs = *D_800A1510; s32 arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; func_80082A14(arg, 1); s0_val = vs; }
hypotheses_tested.result: NO CHANGE versus baseline. With `s0_val = vs;` post-call, `vs` must survive the call (allocated to a callee-save register) and the lever's mechanism — putting the `move s0, vs` in jal delay slot — is lost. Floor returns to 7. Confirms the M5 lever's mechanism IS the delay-slot-fill manipulation (and confirms FAIL: the construct's only effect is on the schedule).

hypotheses_tested.name: M3 / P3 — just `arg` intermediate, no `vs`, `s0_val = *D_800A1510;` direct
hypotheses_tested.result: NO CHANGE. The arg intermediate alone doesn't shift the schedule meaningfully — `addiu a0, a0, 1` ends up at d8 between the chains, NOT in delay slot. The lever requires the `vs` bounce (which IS the cheat construct).

hypotheses_tested.name: Variant sweep negative summary — un-tried session-1 negatives at clean floor-7 baseline
hypotheses_tested.result: NOT exhaustively executed this session. The 30+ variants tested were all in the dual-intermediate-vs-arg family. The session-3 / -4 next_hypotheses #4 — re-testing session-1's measured negatives (operand reassociation, last-arg hoist, merged if/else, inverted branch sense, eager-flag pre-computation) — remains un-executed at clean floor 7 and is the prioritised next-session lever.

discoveries.name: Dual-intermediate dec-order is the only mechanically-effective lever at floor 7 + reorg.c surface, but it's a cheat
discoveries.details: Across 30+ tested variants, ONLY the `{ s32 vs = ...; s32 arg = ...; s0_val = vs; func(arg, 1); }` family (vs-decl-first) moved the floor from 7 to 6. Every other intermediate placement variant stayed at 7. The cheat-reviewer's FAIL is structurally consistent: a lever whose effect depends entirely on LUID-ordering is a codegen-coercion construct, regardless of its outward shape. This NARROWS the lever surface: any pure-C lever for sys_VSync's Region B must either (a) come from outside the dual-intermediate family entirely, or (b) be a single intermediate whose semantic role is REAL (e.g., a sub-computation with a natural name).

discoveries.name: build_insns = 82 IS reachable via clean C only via the rejected family
discoveries.details: All measured ≤7-floor variants have build_insns 81 (one short of target's 82). Only the M5 family produced build_insns 82 — and that family is rejected. So matching the instruction COUNT (a precondition for floor 0) under the constraints of the cheat catalog requires a structurally different lever than M5. The implication: the residual count gap must close via a lever that adds an instruction to the build naturally (e.g., a missing load-delay nop, or making cc1 emit the same operation as two insns instead of one) — without introducing a no-semantic-purpose intermediate.

sessions.lever: No new lever closed; ran BB2_SCHED_DEBUG instrumented cc1 dump on the clean candidate (Region A applied, no inner do-while), and tested two additional named-intermediate variants against the clean floor-7 baseline. Both no-change. Confirmed scan_hand_coded tier=LOW score=0 — TIGHT_C territory, no canonical-asm escape.
sessions.floor_change_note: No change. This session adds 2 more ruled-out hypotheses to the existing exhaustion ledger and surfaces a new observation from the instrumented cc1 dump: cc1's actual emission order DIFFERS from what sched1's pick log claims it placed. The dump shows sched1 places `lw $v0, D_800A1510` (insn 102, LUID 6) at block position 5 and `lw $a0, g_sys_dma_region` (insn 97, LUID 4) at position 6 — i.e. lw v0 BEFORE lw a0. But the cc1 .s emit order has lw a0 BEFORE lw v0. The discrepancy implies a post-sched1 pass (likely mips_reorg or the delay-slot fill in reorg.c) is reshuffling beyond what sched1 records. This means the lever has to bias not just sched1's INSN_PRIORITY (which the named-intermediate variants do test) but ALSO the downstream reorg pass — a smaller surface area for clean C levers.

hypotheses_tested.name: variant — `{ s32 dma = g_sys_dma_region; s0_val = *D_800A1510; func_80082A14(dma + 1, 1); }` (block-hoist of bare g_sys_dma_region declared BEFORE the s0 reload, +1 stays at call site)
hypotheses_tested.result: NO CHANGE versus the clean candidate baseline. Distinct from meta hypothesis 1 (which hoisted dma_arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1, including the +1 — that was WORSE). Bare-load hoist with +1-at-callsite doesn't shift sched1's decision: the addu still gets allocated to the same chain, and the load priority for $a0 chain stays equal to the $v0 chain.

hypotheses_tested.name: variant — `{ s32 dma_arg; s0_val = *D_800A1510; dma_arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; func_80082A14(dma_arg, 1); }` (pre-declared local, late-assigned AFTER s0 reload)
hypotheses_tested.form: { s32 dma_arg; s0_val = *D_800A1510; dma_arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; func_80082A14(dma_arg, 1); }
hypotheses_tested.result: NO CHANGE versus the clean candidate baseline. The pre-decl + late-assign form attempts to give the dma compute a HIGHER LUID than the s0 read (by assigning at use site), hoping sched1 schedules the s0 chain first. No effect — cc1 collapses the trivial local back into the call-site expression at the RTL level (same as the meta's 'function-scope `s32 dma` local' variant). Both named-intermediate forms ruled out; the LUID surface doesn't reach sched1's tie-break for this region.

discoveries.name: scan_hand_coded confirms LOW tier for sys_VSync
discoveries.details: `python3 tools/scan_hand_coded.py --single sys_VSync --json` returned tier=LOW, score=0, with reason 'no strong hand-coded indicators'. All 8 strong signals (s1 uniform multu, s2 empty branch, s3 no spills, s4 front loads, s5 cluster, s6 BIOS jumptable, s7 no save-callee-use, s8 redundant mask shift) returned false. 82-insn function with 3 spills and 6 distinct registers — standard cc1 codegen profile. This confirms TIGHT_C / pure-C territory: no canonical-asm escape applies; the gap must close via C structure.

discoveries.name: sched1's reported layout vs cc1's actual emit DIFFER for Region B
discoveries.details: BB2_SCHED_DEBUG=1 BB2_PRIO_DEBUG=1 on the clean candidate (tmp/gccdbg/cc1, dump at tmp/sched_dump.log). For sys_VSync's main BB (block 9, 12 insns including both calls), sched1 picks (clock 8: insn 102 [lw v0, LUID 6], clock 7: insn 97 [lw a0, LUID 4]) — placing lw v0 at block position 5 and lw a0 at position 6. cc1's actual .s emit (tmp/ings2.s lines 121-130) has lw a0 BEFORE lw v0. The discrepancy implies the delay-slot fill in reorg.c (or mips_reorg) reshuffles after sched1. This explains why named-intermediate variants haven't moved the score: they target sched1's LUID/priority surface, but the reorg pass is the LATER decision point that determines the final emit. A C-level lever for reorg.c's choices would need to target which insn it picks for the jal delay slot — `addu $a0, $a0, 1` (target) vs `li $a1, 1` (build). Currently the build's reorg.c picks li a1 because it's reachable backwards from the jal without crossing a volatile MEM; addu a0+1 is also reachable but is skipped, possibly due to dep-ordering heuristics.

reviewer.evidence: Session 2 invoked the cheat-reviewer on the candidate body (subagent_type: cheat-reviewer). The reviewer FAILed the original (with inner do-while) and instructed removing the inner do-while. After applying that next_action, the candidate body contains: (1) Region A named-base lever — explicitly PASSed by both the prior layer-2 reviewer and this session's reviewer (SOTN named-intermediate family); (2) the Region B spin-wait `volatile s32 *ptr = D_800A1510;` block — explicitly PASSed by this session's reviewer as a standard pointer-to-volatile MMIO spin-wait, NOT in the forbidden volatile-coercion family (which targets `extern volatile T G;` and `*(volatile T *)&G` casts, not local pointer-to-volatile declarations); (3) no inner do-while. New verdict: PASS on the cleaned candidate body. SESSION 4 (2026-06-12): candidate.c UNCHANGED from session 2; the session-2 cheat-reviewer PASS still applies. Session 4 added evidence (permuter findings, all rejected) but did not modify the candidate body.

remaining_gap: 7 (masked) — Region B: second call's volatile-load + arg-setup scheduling. Target keeps the volatile lw of s0 (from D_800A1510) strictly source-ordered adjacent to its pointer load before the func_80082A14(g_sys_dma_region + 1, 1) call; natural cc1 sched1 interleaves it with the independent arg setup. HEAD bridges this with the second regfix reorder rule (TEXT 32-38) PLUS the inner do-while (now removed). Removing the do-while costs 1 instruction (build 81 vs target 82) and adds 2 mismatch positions versus the prior tainted baseline. Session 2 confirmed `hoist-call-arg-local-flips-jal-delay` does NOT apply (documented symptom is a pre-call STORE; this function has a pre-call LOAD). Region B's sched1-priority root cause hypothesis: `lw a0, hi(D_800A2634)` has higher INSN_PRIORITY than `lw v0, hi(D_800A1510)` because its dependent chain reaches the jal (which has high `use` weight in sched.c's `priority()`); the lw v0 / lw s0 chain ends in s0 (callee-save not USEd until post-call). To match target without a sched1 fence, the lw v0 chain needs higher priority — derived from clean C structure rather than a no-semantic-purpose wrap. SESSION 4 (2026-06-12): the WIP's PRIMARY UNPROBED LEVER (decomp-permuter with clean single-function target) was EXECUTED. Random-mode permuter found ONLY cheat-family closing forms (do-while sched1-fence wraps; best non-cheat score = 495 = baseline). Cheat-attraction is strong evidence the structural lever is in reorg.c (delay-slot fill), not at sched1's LUID surface, and random C-level mutations of this candidate don't reach the reorg.c decision point. Next reachable avenues: (a) directed PERM_* macros (PERM_LINESWAP / PERM_VAR / PERM_TYPECAST scripted into base.c) to explore mutations targeting reorg.c rather than random sched1-fence; (b) re-test session-1's measured negatives against the new floor-7 baseline (operand reassociation, last-arg hoist, merged if/else, inverted branch, eager-flag pre-computation — un-tested at clean baseline); (c) instrument reorg.c with BB2_REORG_DEBUG to dump the delay-slot fill candidate set + pick rationale (diagnostic only per [[no-compiler-divergence]]).
rejected_forms: Outer `do { } while (0);` between `s0_val = *D_800A1510;` and the second func_80082A14 call — sched1 fence, layer-2 FAIL on commit 4dd017d8 (see rejected/do-while-sched1-fence.c).

INHERITED inner `do { } while (0);` at src/ings2.c line 69 / candidate.c line 30 (commit d7c91eba 2026-04-09) — sched1 fence (same family as the outer one). Session-2 cheat-reviewer FAIL. Cannot be re-introduced in any form to defeat sched1.

Block-hoist of `s32 dma_arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1;` declared FIRST in a block surrounding the s0 reload + call — measured WORSE against the with-cheat baseline (5->7, lost 1 insn). hoist-call-arg-local-flips-jal-delay rule does not apply: it's documented for pre-call STORE fill, not pre-call LOAD scheduling.

Shared `volatile s32 *ptr = D_800A1510;` declared pre-call, reused post-call — catastrophic (5->23, lost 3 insns). Caches the pointer in a callee-save across the call, eliminating target's fresh post-call reload.

Bare-load hoist `{ s32 dma = g_sys_dma_region; s0_val = *D_800A1510; func_80082A14(dma + 1, 1); }` (session 3) — no change at clean floor 7. The +1-at-callsite split doesn't shift sched1's interleave decision.

Pre-decl + late-assign `{ s32 dma_arg; s0_val = *D_800A1510; dma_arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; func_80082A14(dma_arg, 1); }` (session 3) — no change at clean floor 7. cc1 collapses the trivial local back to the call-site expression; LUID bias doesn't reach the relevant tie-break.

PERMUTER session 4: `do { func_80082A14(g_sys_dma_region + 1, 1); } while (0);` — sched1 fence around the second call (permuter best score 40). Same family as the session-2 FAIL. See rejected/permuter-do-while-around-call.c.

PERMUTER session 4: 4 additional do-while-wrap variants at scores 50, 300, 355, 455 — all same sched1-fence family (wrapping different statement ranges) or semantically broken (455 reuses s0_val incorrectly). All FAIL.

SESSION 5 (M5 + 9 isomorphs): dual named-intermediate `{ s32 vs = *D_800A1510; s32 arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; s0_val = vs; func_80082A14(arg, 1); }` and variants R1/R2/R4/R7/R8/R9/Q5/Q6/Q7 — ALL reach floor 6 (build_insns 82 = target) but cheat-reviewer FAIL: vs is an identity-bounce (no sub-computation to name → NOT the SOTN named-intermediate family); declaration-order sensitivity (only vs-first works) is GCC-LUID-ordering coercion. See rejected/m5-vs-arg-identity-bounce.c for full reviewer reasoning. The whole 'dual-intermediate-with-vs-first' family is ruled out for sys_VSync.
next_hypotheses: SESSION-5 UPDATE — the dual-intermediate-vs-arg lever family (M5 + 9 isomorphs) is RULED OUT (cheat-reviewer FAIL). Floor 6 IS reachable via this family but the reachability is itself cheat-evidence (the lever depends on LUID-ordering). The remaining lever surface is now narrower: any pure-C lever must EITHER come from outside the dual-intermediate family entirely, OR be a single intermediate whose semantic role names a real sub-computation (like Region A's `base = D_800A151C - 1`). Region B's only obvious sub-computation candidate is the +1 (g_sys_dma_region + 1), and a SINGLE-intermediate form `s32 arg = g_sys_dma_region + 1; func(arg, 1);` was measured at floor 7 (M3) — confirming no SOTN-style named-intermediate route exists in Region B without going dual.

SESSION-5 UPDATE — random permuter mode is EXHAUSTED on this candidate (~5 min, ~hundreds of iterations, only cheat-family closing forms found at scores 40/50/300/355 in session 4). The permuter directory permuter/sys_VSync/ from session 4 was NOT preserved across sessions (gitignored); rebuild it for the next session if pursuing the directed-PERM macro route. Next reachable lever via permuter: DIRECTED PERM_* macros. Add PERM_LINESWAP() around Region B's 3 statements, PERM_VAR for the local types, PERM_TYPECAST on the volatile-load result, PERM_ONCE/PERM_FORCE_FORWARD_DECL on the call args. Then re-run permuter — directed mutations may reach the reorg.c delay-slot fill surface that random mutations don't. Any closing form MUST be vetted against the cheat catalog per [[no-new-park-categories]] before commit.

RTL diagnosis per [[register-alloc-pure-c]]: session-3 ran BB2_SCHED_DEBUG=1 BB2_PRIO_DEBUG=1 and stored output at tmp/sched_dump.log (6994 lines). Key finding: sched1's claimed pick order DIFFERS from cc1's actual emit (lw v0 placed at pos 5 in sched1 but emits as pos 6). The decision point is in reorg.c's delay-slot fill, NOT sched1. A focused dump of reorg.c's choices (would need adding BB2_REORG_DEBUG instrumentation) would identify whether the lever surface is the dependence-cone analysis or the simple-fill heuristic.

Read m2c output for sys_VSync (asm/funcs/sys_VSync.s) — session-3 confirmed m2c's reconstruction matches the candidate structure exactly (s0_val read between calls, dma_region + 1 inline call arg). No structural deviation to exploit there.

Re-examine matched siblings in src/ings2.c (sys_SetVsyncMode, sys_SetTimer, motion_make_table, func_80082C58) for an analogous volatile-read + call structure that DID match without a sched1 fence. None obvious on first reading, but a focused .greg dump comparison might reveal the differentiator — e.g., target's pre-call USE pattern for the loaded value.

Re-test the session-1 measured negatives (operand reassociation, last-arg hoist, merged if/else, inverted branch, eager-flag pre-computation) against the NEW floor-7 baseline. Their original measurements were against the tainted baseline, so the deltas may differ. Sessions 2-3 have covered 'pre-computed dma arg / last-arg hoist' variants; the others remain un-tested at floor 7.
